description: Flexible sheet material formed from fibres or yarn is 'liru'. Weaving, knitting, felting, and bonding produce fabrics with different structures. 'wetha' (cloth) points to a piece or amount in ordinary handling; fabric keeps the textile construction in view.
articulatory_notes: Lateral 'l' releases into stressed high-front 'i'. A tap or brief trill then carries the voice into final high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: Optional 'themo' sets off a single fabric sample; continuous material needs none. Construction and finish often distinguish two fabrics more usefully than 'liru' by itself.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A woven fabric gives ornament real crossings, tension, and grain. Pattern can follow the way the material was made instead of floating on its surface as an afterthought.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: textile structure whose weave, texture, and drape shape visual work
semantic_domains.creation: fibres or yarn assembled into a usable sheet material
